new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process in which rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol are simply or successively distilled with juniper berries and other aromatic substances (e.g. coriander, angelica root, anise, ginger) to produce gin.

A quality borne by a spirituous beverage indicating that it has been flavoured with juniper berries (and optionally other aromatic substances such as coriander, angelica root, anise or ginger) during distillation, as required for classification as gin.

A quality borne by a beverage indicating that it is a spirituous beverage as defined for CN subheadings 2208 50 11 and 2208 50 19, obtained by distillation of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatic substances.

new_classes.label_de: Gin, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l (KN 2208 50 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin obtained by simple or successive distillations of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatic substances, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less. Only spirituous beverages meeting the definition of gin qualify for this subheading.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the four principal discriminating criteria for CN 2208 50 11: (1) the product is a spirit produced by distillation (GinDistillationProcess, a subclass of SpiritousDistillationProcess); (2) the distillation substrate is rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatic substances (JuniperBerryAromaticQuality); (3) the product qualifies as a 'spirituous beverage' in the sense of subheadings 2208 50 11/19, not merely any distillate (GinSpiritousQuality); and (4) the product is presented in containers holding ≤ 2 l (SmallContainer). A minor gap remains: the legal text notes that 'only spirituous beverages' (implying a minimum alcoholic strength and organoleptic requirement) qualify — the specific minimum alcoholic strength threshold for gin (typically ≥ 37.5% vol per EU spirits regulation) is not formally encoded because no dedicated 'gin minimum strength quality' class exists in the TBox and creating a datatype range restriction on a plain quality class requires an intermediate individual pattern not straightforwardly supported here. The distinction from sibling subheading 2208 50 19 (large container) is fully captured via the SmallContainer restriction. Coverage is therefore assessed at 0.85.
